TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Fresh agent registration negotiates per-module SSMK keys with DS
Steps:
1. Install agent fresh against SSMK-enabled DS
2. Wait for registration to complete
3. Inspect agent logs for KeyShare exchange
4. Inspect agent vault store for per-module keys
EXPECTED RESULT: Registration succeeds; vault contains a distinct key entry for every active service module (AGENT_UTILS
column13: DMS); no single legacy master key present

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Key Share servlet returns per-module keys with valid AES-GCM material
Steps:
1. From DS, force key share by triggering rotation API
2. On agent, watch the KeyShare HTTP call
3. Verify each returned key length and key id (UUID) in vault
EXPECTED RESULT: All returned keys are 32 bytes; each has a unique UUID; vault rows have non-empty isActive and modified time

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Key Share servlet returns HTTP 401 unauthorized
Steps:
1. Manually revoke agent token on DS so /keyShareRequest returns 401
2. Trigger key share from agent
3. Observe agent behavior
EXPECTED RESULT: Agent logs Unauthorized; does NOT retry in tight loop; falls back to existing valid keys; no crash; status shows ""auth needed""

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Key Share servlet returns HTTP 403 rejected
Steps:
1. Configure DS rule to reject this agent UUID
2. Trigger key share
3. Observe behavior over 10 minutes
EXPECTED RESULT: Agent logs ""key share servlet rejected"" once; backs off; no infinite retry; persistent counter incremented in registry

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Key Share servlet returns malformed JSON
Steps:
1. Use proxy to mangle /keyShareRequest response body
2. Trigger key share
3. Inspect agent
EXPECTED RESULT: Agent logs JSON parse error with safe truncation; vault unchanged; previous keys retained

TEST CASE DESCRIPTION: Key Share servlet transient 503 then 200
Steps:
1. Simulate DS returning 503 on first 2 attempts then 200
2. Trigger key share
3. Inspect retry interval
EXPECTED RESULT: Agent retries with backoff (>= 30s gap); 3rd attempt succeeds; new keys stored
